stemHtml: Most of the aviation accidents happen during the approach/landing phase of flight. What is the most common cause?

options.a: Pilots tend to become overload during this phase of flight.
options.b: Confirmation bias, a tendency to seek information that confirms their expectations.
options.c: 'Get-there-itis' because the pilots tend to avoid a go around or a diversion when in bad weather conditions and stick to the initial plan to land.
options.d: Anti-authority bias, a tendency to disregard rules, regulations and view procedures as unnecessary.
